title: Project Report: Production ML Inference Service — Python Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: FastAPI service serving a scikit-learn model with batch prediction, caching, OpenTelemetry, and k6 load tests.
objectives: Load and version ML model with joblib
OpenTelemetry traces and k6 load test
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Advanced)*

**Production ML Inference Service** is a advanced-level end-to-end project for Python Developer. Duration: 4–6 weeks. FastAPI service serving a scikit-learn model with batch prediction, caching, OpenTelemetry, and k6 load tests. Tools: FastAPI, sklearn, Redis, k6.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Load and version ML model with joblib
Objective: Batch and single prediction endpoints
Objective: Redis result cache
Objective: OpenTelemetry traces and k6 load test
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Python Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Python Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Python Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Model
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Model** *(Level: Advanced)*

Train and serialize model. Complete these tasks: sklearn pipeline; MLflow log. Deliverable for this phase: model.pkl v1.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] sklearn pipeline
- [ ] MLflow log
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Model is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Python Developer agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the model.pkl v1 to the teacher.

chapters.title: Phase: Cache
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Cache** *(Level: Advanced)*

Redis for repeated inputs. Complete these tasks: Cache key hash; TTL config. Deliverable for this phase: Cache hit metrics.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Cache key hash
- [ ] TTL config
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

chapters.title: Phase: Load Test
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Load Test** *(Level: Advanced)*

k6 script 1000 RPS. Complete these tasks: p95 latency; Error rate. Deliverable for this phase: k6 HTML report.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] p95 latency
- [ ] Error rate
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Load Test is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 4 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Python Developer agile sprints and coursework milestones.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Advanced)*

Submit all final deliverables: Model artifact; k6 report; Grafana/traces screenshot; API docs. Skills demonstrated: ML serving, Caching, Observability. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Model artifact
Deliverable: k6 report
Deliverable: Grafana/traces screenshot
Deliverable: API docs
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Model artifact and passes the rubric.
